entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 21 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-21 04:00AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • NSW budget LIVE updates: Matt Kean targets stamp duty reform, families in $27b spending spree; $11.3b deficit revealed
  • Queensland increases coal royalties, insists no promise is broken
  • Queensland budget: Govt to levy more than $1b from big business for mental health
  • Nude Tuesday review: Offbeat naked romp more tragedy than comedy
  • Teachers to hold ‘unprecedented’ strike as anger over shortages, pay boils over
  • Dave thought fatherhood would be wonderful, instead it made him depressed
  • Cruel Intentions: The '90s Musical
  • Australia news LIVE: Federal budget could fall off ‘debt cliff’; Researchers say FINA decision based on ‘opinion, not science’
  • Kings School defends regatta trip, says business class trips with spouses are standard
  • Lawyers for man accused of raping Brittany Higgins make bid to delay trial
  • Judge blasts Wilkinson’s Logies speech about Higgins
  • Beyonce releases new music from her first solo album since 2016
  • Police appeal for information following Braybrook armed robbery
  • A daring but necessary exhibition that shows what Australian art is missing
  • Ex-Crown boss Rob Rankin sells Woollahra trophy home for $35 million
  • Grade-A schoolgirl admits role in teacher murder plot
  • Bigger than Titanic? Top Gun: Maverick flies through Australian box office records
  • NSW Treasurer Matt Kean gets a surprise visit from son
  • HG Nelson cracks up rugby greats
  • Behind the scenes: 24 hours at the Logies
  • Home buyers warned by RBA to expect more interest rate pain
  • Best of cartoons, June 21, 2022
  • Budget faces debt cliff as interest rates on borrowings soar
  • Competition watchdog to investigate potential gaming of energy market
  • Coalition’s $1 billion energy promise fails to power up
  • Labor’s environment reforms will take time to deliver, Plibersek says
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • MPs back quiet diplomacy in Assange case
  • Slow progress on vaccine deliveries to developing countries
  • Soldiers waiting up to eight weeks to see a psychiatrist: Royal Commission
  • Palmer backs Babet to hold major parties to account
  • Falling prices not enough to make housing more affordable: Moody’s
  • ‘Fat and ugly’: Comment allegedly made by Dawson allowed into evidence
  • Explanation sought for $25m price tag to fly Aboriginal flag on Harbour Bridge
  • ‘It’s quite a touchy subject’: Plans to transform Circular Quay frustrate business owners
  • Public had a right to know what Assange exposed
  • In the Herald: June 21, 1949
  • Where there’s smoke ...
  • High-salary, key teaching roles must be part of education reform
  • Sydney teenager dies in Thredbo skiing accident
  • Perrottet is proving an interesting premier, but the mistakes must stop
  • Circular Quay in disrepair
  • Winter solstice
  • ‘Breathtaking double standard’: Sebastian withheld money too, court told
  • Chris Dawson said wife Lynette left him to join commune, court hears
  • Student accommodation like we’ve never seen before wins praise
  • ‘Complete and utter fabrication’: Chris Dawson disputes hitman claim
  • Before chickens were tasty, they were sacred pets
  • Unite to resist China or ‘get bullied one by one’: last HK governor
  • Antarctic researchers mark solstice with icy dip
  • Hong Kong’s iconic Jumbo Floating Restaurant capsizes at sea
  • Six hospitalised after NYC taxi jumps curb
  • US Senate candidate's campaign ad pulled from Facebook
  • Taliban frees ex-BBC cameraman, four others, after ‘long ordeal’
  • Israel heads to polls for fifth time in three years
  • We can’t catch them all: Sri Lankan navy on boats to Australia
  • I was blacklisted by Putin, but I’m no Russophobe
  • Disappearances ‘show Rwanda unfit to host Commonwealth summit’
  • Rapidly spreading bird flu infects foxes and other wild mammals
  • Macron hunts for way to salvage a ruling majority after support surges for far-left, far-right
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Protester gets 26 months’ jail after attacks on police officer and horse
  • Heavy focus on women, stamp duty and cost of living in NSW budget
  • NSW woman dies in golf buggy accident
  • Interest rate rise expected
  • Man dies in Blue Mountains house fire
  • Victorian government announces landmark mental health reform
  • Homicide investigation launched in Queensland
  • Qantas scraps mask requirements
  • Adam Bandt's Australian flag stunt slammed
  • Man stabbed to death, home set on fire
  • Unhappy creditor pursues wind-up of flexible work firm
  • ASX rises at open as it bids to end losing streak
  • China is trying to revive its economy. We just got a sign it is not going to plan
  • RBA suffered ‘reputational damage’ from messy COVID policy exit
  • Qantas scraps masks for some international flights
  • Trust me, you won’t use that new function on your iPhone
  • The coin that could wreck crypto
  • He was once America’s richest man. Now he has lost his crown jewel
  • The four factors that help women combine paid work with having children
  • ‘One and done’: Moderna could make combined COVID-19, flu jab in Melbourne
  • ‘Totally the underdog’: Inside Mr Yum’s $100m global expansion plans
  • ‘Not worth the risk’: ATO warns on home offices, COVID payments and ‘side hustles’
  • Woodside, Fortescue in race to pick up NZ green hydrogen project
  • PointsBet soars on $94m injection by financial trading giant SIG
  • ‘Double whammy’: Super funds are set for their worst year since the GFC
  • The Wrap: ASX falls for seventh straight session as resources stocks decline
  • Star executives ‘buried’ scandal in board paper
  • ‘There’s so much fear’: A crypto winter has arrived
  • ‘Investment guinea pig’: Why US billionaire Yass is punting on PointsBet
  • Feel-good hit Only Murders makes a marvellous return to TV
  • A tribute to Cook Suck: Australia’s favourite and deeply unhinged food blog
  • TV’s night of whites: Why are the Logie Awards taking so long to catch up on diversity?
  • ‘Four Oscars can’t be wrong’: Formal dress shopping with Catherine Martin
  • ‘It’s always worth having fun’: Hamish Blake’s guide to the good life
  • Cattle calls, stress and high kicks: Scrapping for work in the capital of dance
  • A ripping yarn but can we trust The Offer’s view of Hollywood history?
  • National weather forecast for Tuesday June 21
  • Oil spills into Timor Sea from troubled Montara field off WA coast
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • George Lamming, renowned Caribbean novelist, dies at 94
  • Kremlin says captured Americans are ‘soldiers of fortune,’ not subject to Geneva Conventions
  • As Latin America embraces a new left, the U.S. could take a back seat
  • Second American is reported killed in Ukraine fighting
  • Filipino government lawyer fatally shot while vacationing in U.S.
  • Hong Kong’s landmark Jumbo floating restaurant sinks at sea
  • Britain will electronically tag some asylum seekers with GPS devices
  • Spain devastated by wildfires amid record-breaking heat wave
  • Millions stranded as heavy flooding sweeps through Bangladesh and India
  • Israel’s leader to dissolve Knesset, triggering new elections
  • Former journalist among five British men released from Taliban custody
  • Israel is hoping its gas will finally make it part of the Middle East
  • Sri Lanka shuts down, families struggle for food as crisis deepens
  • In major blow, Macron loses absolute majority in French Parliament
  • Last month, China imported more Russian crude oil than ever before
  • Ukraine awaits E.U. candidate decision; leaders prepare for long war
  • Cambodian catches world's largest recorded freshwater fish
  • Mexican diplomat starts jostling for 2024 nomination
  • Hong Kong's iconic Jumbo Floating Restaurant capsizes at sea
  • Colombian voters elect country's first Black vice president
  • Gucci unveils 'HaHaHa' collaboration with Harry Styles
  • Petro faces challenges to deliver Colombians promised change
  • Man who coordinated Honduras activist's murder sentenced
  • Israel to dissolve parliament, call 5th election in 3 years
  • Mali government says jihadi rebels kill 132 civilians
  • Speaker at meeting of Ginni Thomas group called Biden’s win illegitimate long after Jan. 6, video shows
  • Wildfire in New Jersey may become largest in 15 years, officials say
  • Miami police want to buy Floridians’ guns — and send them to Ukraine
  • Investors paid thousands for rare wines. It was a scam, feds say.
  • Held at gunpoint behind the wheel, a driver sped to alert officers
  • Louisville mayor punched by man in second attack on local Democrats
  • Floridians give DeSantis points for his covid stance. Will it hold?
  • Why this teacher keeps one chair empty in his middle school classroom
  • This Texas teen wanted an abortion. She now has twins.
  • Bats and balls — but not baseball — win over Texas kids
  • Bitcoin bounces back above $20,000 after weekend slide
  • Oil refineries are making a windfall. Why do they keep closing?
  • Why gas is so expensive in some U.S. states but not others
  • Twitch ad update offers some streamers big money, others pocket change
  • Apple Store workers approve union, the first in the U.S.
  • A picture-perfect metaverse is years away. Meta’s prototypes prove it.
  • Amazon drones are coming to town. Some locals want to shoot them.
  • How to hire a reliable roofing contractor for your home
  • Association requiring owner to upgrade roof to match aesthetic changes
  • Two-bedroom condo on Connecticut Avenue NW lists for $350,000
  • For Malaysia, This Is No Game of Chicken
  • The Centuries-Old Financial System Better Than DeFi
  • The Death of the WTO Has Only Been Delayed
  • The West Is Facing a Followership Crisis
  • How an Emboldened Far-Right Is Changing French Politics
  • How Europe Became So Dependent on Putin for Its Gas
  • Why the ECB Needs New Tools for Bond ‘Fragmentation’
  • How to Access the UK Cost-of-Living Support Package
  • The most memorable lines from the Jan. 6 hearings so far
  • Our picks for Tuesday’s D.C. Democratic primary
  • How Biden can convince us that recession isn’t 'inevitable’
  • Texas Republicans want to secede? Good riddance.
  • To stop climate change, regulate carbon as a toxic substance
  • The mission from which Pope Francis should never retire
  • Pediatric covid vaccines could change how we deal with the pandemic
  • How the GOP is making national policy one state at a time
  • A generation of homeowners encounters a strange new market
  • This isn’t just Ukraine’s war. It’s our war, too. Act accordingly.
  • The center has not held in Colombia, but democracy still might
  • Joe Manchin is choking off one of America’s best anti-poverty programs
  • Be careful about lionizing Jan. 6 witnesses. They failed to stop the insurrection.
  • Elite Republicans are now openly encouraging political violence
  • Why Watergate-era campaign finance laws have failed
  • Finally, ‘Top Gun’ has a female pilot. Real life should catch up.
  • As an adoptee, I know: Adoption is not a fairy-tale answer to abortion
  • Cartoon by Jeff Danziger
  • Three takeaways from France’s legislative elections — and what could be next
  • Coexistence is not equality
  • U.S. policies are punishing Afghan women and children
  • How to end the war in Ukraine
  • Let’s not take our eyes off the true ‘reality’
  • It is time for leadership from Republicans on guns
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia’s Blockade of Ukraine Is ‘War Crime,’ Top E.U. Official Says
  • Russia threatens to retaliate against Lithuania, a NATO member, over restrictions on shipments to Kaliningrad.
  • Representatives from Finland and Sweden meet with Turkish officials to discuss NATO membership.

  • Dr. Paul M. Ellwood Jr., Architect of the H.M.O., Is Dead at 95

News from the New York Times

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Nomura names Nanavati as Southeast Asia investment banking head

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Swine fever puts meats from Italy, Germany off menus in Japan
  • 7,000 holdouts have yet to give back their COVID subsidies
  • Big aftershock hits Ishikawa after quake that injured 6
  • Survivor of Battle of Okinawa sees parallels with war in Ukraine
  • ICAN leader blasts Japan for skipping TPNW meeting
  • Kishida leaning toward budget austerity ahead of election
  • Japan’s NTT to begin phasing in remote work as the ‘new normal’
  • Top companies worry the weak yen is hurting Japan’s economy
  • Spark plug maker set to change name in drive for greener image
  • IT industry group struggles with Rakuten head at the helm
  • Lawyers call on intl court to investigate Uighur treatment
  • China’s 1st Horn of Africa envoy offers to mediate in region
  • China’s May oil imports from Russia soar 55% to a record, surpass Saudi supply
  • ‘It always wins’: North Korea may declare COVID-19 victory
  • For K-pop supergroup BTS, questions remain about its future
  • BTS break sparks debate on activism, military exemptions
  • Myanmar’s Suu Kyi celebrates birthday with cake in court
  • Cambodian catches world’s largest recorded freshwater fish
  • Asian markets mostly lower ahead of U.S. holiday
  • New park in central Bangkok gives Thais needed green space
  • As Thailand greenlights cannabis, patients welcome cheaper supplies
  • Russian journalist sells Nobel Prize for Ukrainian children
  • French election: Macron loses absolute majority in parliament in ‘democratic shock’
  • Autonomous robot delivers the goods to homes in Tsukuba
  • Musician Yuzo Kayama to retire from the stage after 61 years
  • ‘Golden Kamuy’ walks off with Grand Prize at cartoonist awards
  • Lisa’s Wanderings Around Japan/Ise Jingu: Finding divine grace along with hospitality at a grand shrine
  • EDITORIAL: Economic impact of yen’s plunge an urgent task for BOJ
  • VOX POPULI: The great closure of bookstores nationwide bodes ill for humanity
  • VOX POPULI: Opaque systems make it hard to trust restaurant review websites
  • INTERVIEW/ TPNW signatories meeting chair: Reminding world of threat as nuke danger ‘gravest’ in decades
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 21 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-21 03:03AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Before the storm (2007-2012): Hong Kong’s national pride and a rare political deal in 25 photos
  • Former Hong Kong leader CY Leung says American Express apologised to wife Regina after suing her over allegedly unpaid HK$93,155 credit card bill
  • Satire in Hong Kong: ‘You have to laugh, or you will cry,’ says political cartoonist Harry Harrison
  • Anti-terrorism campaign urges Hong Kong hardware store owners, chemical suppliers to report suspicious customers
  • Ex-Hong Kong governor: China breached city autonomy pledge ‘comprehensively’
  • Hong Kong Palace Museum to act as ‘window’ for promoting Chinese culture, director of Beijing counterpart says
  • Hong Kong’s iconic Jumbo Floating Restaurant sinks in South China Sea after encountering adverse conditions
  • Travel disruption to spill into first quarter of 2023, head of global airline association warns amid labour shortage and rising demand
  • Hong Kong could start quarantine-free business travel corridor with mainland China, fight for more flexible rules on border reopening, advisers urge
  • 1 in 10 Hong Kong kindergartens warn of possible closure in coming years as enrolment declines
  • Hong Kong government to give eligible elderly receiving allowances an extra HK$995 a month from September 1
  • Hong Kong Bar Association head stresses importance of ‘public perception’ after incoming deputy justice minister retains party membership
  • Debate over whether Hong Kong was indeed British colony reflects lack of education on Chinese constitution, lawmakers say
  • Hong Kong film star Louis Koo sees G Storm poster image misappropriated by Chinese NFT platform
  • ‘Spicy policies’ helped fortify Hong Kong’s market, save homeowners from negative-equity trap, defaults, ex-HKMA CEO Chan says
  • Coronavirus: hotel quarantine for top Hong Kong officials, advisers and lawmakers cut to 1 day ahead of July 1 celebrations; city logs 1,186 local cases
  • Hong Kong’s interbank rate climbs to two-year high as HKMA’s intervention saps liquidity from city’s currency market
  • Wealth gap between Hong Kong’s crazy rich, miserably poor widens since handover
  • Hong Kong’s environmental problems, from plastic pollution to filthy air to food waste, and the people fighting them
  • Teen girl in China steals US$18,000 meant for sick mother’s operation to go on a shopping spree
  • China’s political party school in Africa takes first students from 6 countries
  • China’s Horn of Africa envoy tells regional peace conference he is ready to mediate disputes
  • European nations tell China ‘self-isolation’ is stoking ‘mistrust’ in plea for easing of Covid-19 curbs
  • Chinese fugitive wanted for racist videos of Malawi children arrested in Zambia
  • China’s Mars mission on track to lead the world in retrieving Martian rocks by 2031, says programme veteran
  • Mass evacuations as record rain, floods strike southern China
  • Cop 15: UN Biodiversity Conference set to move from China to Canada after Covid-19 delays
  • China’s anti-corruption drive brings tighter rules for officials’ families to stop them exploiting their connections
  • China’s Russian oil imports surged over 50 per cent in May, boosted by discounts amid Ukraine war sanctions
  • China says latest anti-ballistic missile test ‘achieved objective’
  • US and Chinese businesses should team up to tackle climate change, says former US treasury secretary Paulson
  • What’s the key to stopping Covid overload of health systems in China? Focus on the vulnerable, study finds
  • Tencent news aggregator app Kuai Bao to cease operations next month, as China’s Big Tech firms cut noncore businesses
  • Chinese police make arrests in Henan banking scandal that has put spotlight on financial risks
  • China’s Covid mass testing regime is creating a sea of medical waste
  • China’s tattoo ban for minors criticised as circular logic and irrational fear of difference, but many welcome new rules
  • Taiwan’s Thunderbolt-2000 rocket system explodes during live-combat training
  • China’s coronavirus controls top concern for European firms, who may ‘vote with their feet’ if uncertainty persists
  • European students on the brink, locked out by China’s zero-Covid rules
  • A new Quad? Japan, South Korea, Australia, New Zealand eye four-way anti-China summit on sidelines of Nato meet
  • Indonesian Islamist group Khilafatul Muslimin with ‘hundreds of thousands’ of members wants to build caliphate
  • World’s biggest freshwater fish caught by Cambodian in the Mekong River
  • Japan court upholds gay marriage ban, saying it is constitutional
  • Rice job: Philippines’ Bongbong Marcos appoints himself agriculture chief, plans ‘impossible’ rice price slash
  • Airlines restoring flights faster than planned to meet demand
  • Should Philippines’ Marcos be ‘on guard’ as Sara Duterte is sworn in early, meets China envoy?
  • East China Sea tensions: Will fresh Sino-Japan talks over Beijing’s gas drilling end in deadlock again?
  • Indonesia looks to Singapore to offload its surplus chickens
  • Fears for judo’s future in Japan as bullied, burnt-out children quit in droves
  • Three generations of Filipino-New Zealand family killed in ‘shocking’ crash
  • Filipino lawyer shot dead while on US holiday with mother: ‘I will bring him home soon in a box’
  • Belgium returns Congo independence hero Patrice Lumumba’s tooth to family
  • Elon Musk’s 18-year-old trans child seeks name change to sever ties with father
  • As Russia closes its doors on academics, Central Asian universities have an opportunity to expand their networks
  • Despite Western weapons, Ukraine is outgunned in the east
  • Ukraine war: US and allies mulling Russian oil price cap, Treasury chief Janet Yellen says
  • Ukraine war: Russia says captured US fighters must be ‘held responsible for their crimes’
  • Hopes fade for swift Nato accession for Finland and Sweden
  • Israel facing fifth election in three years after fragile coalition collapses
  • Hollywood star Ben Stiller visiting Ukraine says ‘seeking safety is a right’
  • Russian journalist sells Nobel Peace Prize medal for record US$103.5 million to aid Ukrainian children
  • Ukraine’s Zelensky expects Russia attacks to intensify as EU decision looms
  • Hong Kong stocks head for longest winning streak in three weeks on China recovery optimism, as property and casino stocks rally
  • Kowloon may attract more Chinese firms as office-rental cost gap narrows between Greater Bay Area cities and Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong developers lower prices to generate sales amid interest rate hikes, high supply
  • New Zealand’s housing reforms lead the way on tackling affordability and speculation
  • China’s national ambitions have become untethered from personal aspirations in the name of zero Covid
  • Fear grips cryptocurrency traders and magnifies the global rout in US$247 billion DeFi market, Binance’s founder CZ says
  • British MPs call for Wimbledon to drop HSBC as a sponsor over endorsement of Hong Kong’s national security law
  • US-China trade truce is needed to lift the global economic gloom
  • Chinese game studio XD makes US a priority in its overseas expansion drive as home market becomes increasingly difficult to navigate
  • Chinese court rules car sale made using digital currency invalid as Beijing’s unrelenting crypto crackdown continues
  • Why use of social media to market fashion in China and West is so different – the hard sell from influencers doesn’t work in US or Europe … yet
  • Taboo: how the word came to describe topics such as menstruation and race, and how it helped one tourist hotspot achieve zero Covid
  • What do we see when dying? How a psychedelic trip can trigger at life’s end, and why we need to be better prepared for it
  • Jongga, No. 1 kimchi brand in Korea since 1987, innovates the country’s essential food worldwide
  • Science fiction is best placed to discuss climate crisis, says Chen Qiufan, Chinese sci-fi author
  • K-drama Jinxed at First: Seohyun, Na In-woo in cutesy fantasy romcom that comes on a little strong
  • Prince William’s style at 40: how his fashion choices have changed under Kate’s influence and the brands he now loves
  • Inside Bollywood legend Jackie Shroff’s US$4 million Mumbai family home: he lives in a luxurious eight-bedroom flat with Tiger, Krishna and Ayesha – and counts Rani Mukerji as a neighbour
  • Inside tech billionaire Larry Ellison’s US$160 million superyacht Musashi: the Oracle co-founder’s 88-metre, Japanese-style vessel was even featured on Netflix’s The Dropout
  • 9 of the most famous celebrity twin pairs in Hollywood, from Mary-Kate and Ashley Olsen, and Brangelina’s children Vivienne and Knox Jolie-Pitt, to Khloé Kardashian’s besties Malika and Khadijah Haqq
  • Meet Dylan and Paris Brosnan, Pierce Brosnan’s hunky sons: both are fashion models, working with the likes of Moschino and Karlie Kloss, and were Golden Globes ambassadors in 2020
  • Who are the richest Hunger Games stars? Net worths, ranked – from Oscar winner Jennifer Lawrence and OG actor Woody Harrelson, to rock legend Lenny Kravitz and hunky Hemsworth brother Liam
  • Who is Britney Spears’ new sister-in-law, Fay Asghari? The Iranian-American beauty is a nurse with her own aesthetic clinic in Beverly Hills, and even sat in front of Madonna at brother Sam’s wedding
  • Who is Lucy Guo, richest self-made woman under 40 after Kylie Jenner? The wealthy tech CEO co-founded Scale AI with world’s youngest billionaire Alexandr Wang – and is neighbours with David Beckham
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • EU warns against fossil fuel ‘backsliding’ as coal replaces Russian gas
  • US oil refiners: flight from oil investment has raised pump prices

  • Will boomers cause more inflation?

  • China gaming/NetEase: Winnie the Pooh lapse is honey for the bears
  • No, Russia won’t replace Swift with the blockchain

  • Losing money is par for the course with experienced investors
